briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030. This plan will guide how the BHA develops and manages affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years, which may affect local construction, development, and property management activity.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: A BC municipal council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) for 2026. UBCM is an association that represents BC local governments and advocates on their behalf to provincial and federal governments.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council has awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology. This is a public procurement (government purchasing) decision and does not directly change rules or regulations for private businesses.

briefs.title: Modular four-plex pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue sent back for further review
briefs.summary: City council has sent the contract award for a modular housing four-plex pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue back to staff for additional review. The goal is to find ways to manage the project more efficiently before moving forward.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: A local council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments to these contractors will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price agreed in advance.

briefs.title: $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewed with Blackrete Paving by municipal council
briefs.summary: A local municipal council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government procurement decision (a contract awarded by a public body to a private company) and does not introduce new rules or regulations for small business owners.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application accepted by Council
briefs.summary: City Council has approved staff to accept a rezoning application (a formal request to change how land can be used) for 3856 & 3870 Carrigan Court. The proposed development would include a mix of commercial and residential units — both strata (individually owned) and rental — and will now move forward for further review by Council.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Council meeting agendas for the public hearing date on this rezoning application.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Grand Promenade towers rezoned: 362 new strata units added, hotel replaces office space
briefs.summary: Council has approved steps to rezone the towers at 3900 Grand Promenade. The plan removes commercial office space and replaces it with a hotel and restaurant, adds 362 new strata (privately owned) residential units, and sets up a new Housing Agreement for the nearby Carrigan site.
briefs.action_required: If your business occupies or plans to use space at 3900 Grand Promenade, review how this rezoning affects your lease or plans.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Building at 6535 Kingsway Moves Closer to Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for 6535 Kingsway. If approved, the bylaw would allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space on that site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the rezoning bylaw vote and any public hearing notice.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility Rezoning at 2757 Norland Avenue Moves Forward
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how a piece of land can be used) for 2757 Norland Avenue to allow a five-storey self-storage building. No public hearing is required because the proposal already lines up with the city's Official Community Plan (the long-term land use blueprint for Burnaby).
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 2757 Norland Avenue, monitor the rezoning bylaw adoption for any impact on your operations.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council endorses Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan and provincial funding advocacy authorized
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy and given staff the green light to build an implementation plan alongside community partners. The Mayor has also been directed to write to the provincial government asking for funding to support poverty reduction efforts in the city.

briefs.title: City Council receives 2025 investment year-end report and 2026 forecast — no action required
briefs.summary: City Council has received and reviewed the 2025 Investment Program year-end report along with the 2026 investment forecast, both prepared by the Financial Management Committee. This was an information item only — no new rules, fees, or requirements were introduced for businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project now underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received the project initiation overview for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. An OCP is a long-term blueprint that guides how land is used and how the city grows — this action plan sets out the steps to put that blueprint into practice.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 5058 Imperial Street — zoning bylaw amendment passes first two readings
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific piece of land can be used) for 5058 Imperial Street. The vote was not unanimous — four council members opposed it. The bylaw must still pass a third reading and final adoption before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, attend upcoming council meetings to monitor the bylaw's progress.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 6800 Lougheed Highway — what nearby property owners should know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific piece of land can be used) for 6800 Lougheed Highway. This means the property is moving through the formal approval process to allow a different type of development or land use at that address.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's public hearing schedule to attend or submit comments before the rezoning is finalized.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue — first and second reading passed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the first and second reading of a zoning amendment (a change to the rules about how land can be used or developed) for two properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This moves the rezoning application forward in the approval process, but a final decision has not yet been made.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near Springer Avenue, monitor future Council meetings for the final reading and approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates rules for parking meters and EV charging meters
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment (an update) to its Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019. This change updates the rules that govern how parking meters and EV (electric vehicle) charging meters operate in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw for compliance.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its fees and charges bylaw — costs for city services may have changed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2, 2026 to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This bylaw sets the prices the City charges for permits, licences, inspections, and other municipal services — so if your business relies on any of these, the amounts you pay may now be different.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to confirm what you now owe for any permits, licences, or city services.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its bylaw notice enforcement rules — fines and notices may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2, 2026 to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This bylaw sets out how the City issues notices and collects fines when businesses or property owners break local rules. The specific changes were not detailed in the announcement, but any update to this bylaw can affect how violations are handled and what penalties apply.
briefs.action_required: Check with the City of Burnaby to find out exactly what changed and if your business is affected.
briefs.industries: Retail, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: 190 Residents Oppose Rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street — Petition Received by Council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received a petition signed by 190 residents opposing the rezoning (a request to change how land can be used) of 5058 Imperial Street. The petition was submitted by neighbours from 5051 Imperial Street and the surrounding area, and Council has noted it for information.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor Council meetings for rezoning decision updates.
